title: Velvety Sweet Korean Flower Hwajeon
description: A savory and tender Korean dish from the Gyeongsang region, featuring ground nut oil and score-fat, making it paleo-friendly and kid-friendly.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, sugar, and Korean chili flakes.
Add the water and egg to the bowl and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Heat the ground nut oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Using a spoon, drop small amounts of the batter into the oil and flatten slightly.
Cook the hwajeon for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the hwajeon from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the hwajeon warm, garnished with toasted sesame seeds and a drizzle of score-fat.
